verses.latin_vulgate: Habitavit autem Jacob in terra Chanaan, in qua pater suus peregrinatus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֣שֶׁב יַעֲקֹ֔ב בְּאֶ֖רֶץ מְגוּרֵ֣י אָבִ֑יו בְּאֶ֖רֶץ כְּנָֽעַן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob dwelt in the land of Chanaan, wherein his father sojourned.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob asui sillä maalla, jossa hänen isänsä muukalainen oli ollut: (nimittäin) Kanaanin maalla.
verses.king_james: And Jacob dwelt in the land wherein his father was a stranger, in the land of Canaan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hæ sunt generationes ejus : Joseph cum sedecim esset annorum, pascebat gregem cum fratribus suis adhuc puer : et erat cum filiis Balæ et Zelphæ uxorum patris sui : accusavitque fratres suos apud patrem crimine pessimo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֣לֶּה ׀ תֹּלְד֣וֹת יַעֲקֹ֗ב יוֹסֵ֞ף בֶּן־שְׁבַֽע־עֶשְׂרֵ֤ה שָׁנָה֙ הָיָ֨ה רֹעֶ֤ה אֶת־אֶחָיו֙ בַּצֹּ֔אן וְה֣וּא נַ֗עַר אֶת־בְּנֵ֥י בִלְהָ֛ה וְאֶת־בְּנֵ֥י זִלְפָּ֖ה נְשֵׁ֣י אָבִ֑יו וַיָּבֵ֥א יוֹסֵ֛ף אֶת־דִּבָּתָ֥ם רָעָ֖ה אֶל־אֲבִיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And these are his generations: Joseph, when he was sixteen years old, was feeding the flock with his brethren, being but a boy: and he was with the sons of Bala and of Zelpha his father's wives: and he accused his brethren to his father of a most wicked crime.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja nämät ovat Jakobin sukukunnat: Joseph oli seitsemäntoistakymmenen ajastaikainen, koska hän kaitsi karjaa veljeinsä kanssa, ja hän oli nuorella ijällänsä Bilhan ja Silpan, isänsä emäntäin lasten kanssa: ja Joseph ilmoitti isällensä, mikä paha sanoma heistä kuului.
verses.king_james: These are the generations of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was feeding the flock with his brethren; and the lad was with the sons of Bilhah, and with the sons of Zilpah, his father’s wives: and Joseph brought unto his father their evil report.

verses.latin_vulgate: Israël autem diligebat Joseph super omnes filios suos, eo quod in senectute genuisset eum : fecitque ei tunicam polymitam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל אָהַ֤ב אֶת־יוֹסֵף֙ מִכׇּל־בָּנָ֔יו כִּֽי־בֶן־זְקֻנִ֥ים ה֖וּא ל֑וֹ וְעָ֥שָׂה ל֖וֹ כְּתֹ֥נֶת פַּסִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now Israel loved Joseph above all his sons, because he had him in his old age: and he made him a coat of divers colours.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Israel rakasti Josephia enemmin kuin kaikkia muita lapsiansa, että hän oli syntynyt hänelle vanhalla ijällänsä: ja teki hänelle kirjavan hameen.
verses.king_james: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his children, because he was the son of his old age: and he made him a coat of many colours.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videntes autem fratres ejus quod a patre plus cunctis filiis amaretur, oderant eum, nec poterant ei quidquam pacifice loqui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּרְא֣וּ אֶחָ֗יו כִּֽי־אֹת֞וֹ אָהַ֤ב אֲבִיהֶם֙ מִכׇּל־אֶחָ֔יו וַֽיִּשְׂנְא֖וּ אֹת֑וֹ וְלֹ֥א יָכְל֖וּ דַּבְּר֥וֹ לְשָׁלֹֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And his brethren seeing that he was loved by his father, more than all his sons, hated hem, and could not speak peaceably to him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin hänen veljensä näkivät, että hänen isänsä rakasti häntä enemmin kuin kaikkia hänen veljiänsä, vihasivat he häntä; ja ei voineet puhutella häntä ystävällisesti.
verses.king_james: And when his brethren saw that their father loved him more than all his brethren, they hated him, and could not speak peaceably unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accidit quoque ut visum somnium referret fratribus suis : quæ causa majoris odii seminarium fuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּחֲלֹ֤ם יוֹסֵף֙ חֲל֔וֹם וַיַּגֵּ֖ד לְאֶחָ֑יו וַיּוֹסִ֥פוּ ע֖וֹד שְׂנֹ֥א אֹתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now it fell out also that he told his brethren a dream, that he had dreamed: which occasioned them to hate him the more.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Joseph näki unen, ja ilmoitti sen veljillensä, josta he rupesivat häntä enemmin vihaamaan.
verses.king_james: And Joseph dreamed a dream, and he told it his brethren: and they hated him yet the more.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ad eos : Audite somnium meum quod vidi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר אֲלֵיהֶ֑ם שִׁמְעוּ־נָ֕א הַחֲל֥וֹם הַזֶּ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר חָלָֽמְתִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to them: Hear my dream which I dreamed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä hän oli sanonut heille, kuulkaas tätä unta, jonka minä näin.
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Hear, I pray you, this dream which I have dreamed:

verses.latin_vulgate: putabam nos ligare manipulos in agro : et quasi consurgere manipulum meum, et stare, vestrosque manipulos circumstantes adorare manipulum meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֠הִנֵּ֠ה אֲנַ֜חְנוּ מְאַלְּמִ֤ים אֲלֻמִּים֙ בְּת֣וֹךְ הַשָּׂדֶ֔ה וְהִנֵּ֛ה קָ֥מָה אֲלֻמָּתִ֖י וְגַם־נִצָּ֑בָה וְהִנֵּ֤ה תְסֻבֶּ֙ינָה֙ אֲלֻמֹּ֣תֵיכֶ֔ם וַתִּֽשְׁתַּחֲוֶ֖יןָ לַאֲלֻמָּתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I thought we were binding sheaves in the field: and my sheaf arose as it were, and stood, and your sheaves standing about bowed down before my sheaf.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, me olimme sitovanamme jalallisia vainiolla ja minun jalalliseni nousi ja seisoi; ja katso, teidän jalallisenne seisoivat ympärillä, ja kumarsivat minun jalallistani.
verses.king_james: For, behold, we were binding sheaves in the field, and, lo, my sheaf arose, and also stood upright; and, behold, your sheaves stood round about, and made obeisance to my sheaf.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt fratres ejus : Numquid rex noster eris ? aut subjiciemur ditioni tuæ ? Hæc ergo causa somniorum atque sermonum, invidiæ et odii fomitem ministravit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמְרוּ לוֹ֙ אֶחָ֔יו הֲמָלֹ֤ךְ תִּמְלֹךְ֙ עָלֵ֔ינוּ אִם־מָשׁ֥וֹל תִּמְשֹׁ֖ל בָּ֑נוּ וַיּוֹסִ֤פוּ עוֹד֙ שְׂנֹ֣א אֹת֔וֹ עַל־חֲלֹמֹתָ֖יו וְעַל־דְּבָרָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: His brethren answered: Shalt thou be our king? or shall we be subject to thy dominion? Therefore this matter of his dreams and words ministered nourishment to their envy and hatred.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoivat hänen veljensä hänelle: sinäkö tulet meidän kuninkaaksemme, kokonansa hallitsemaan meitä? Ja vihasivat häntä vielä enemmin, hänen unensa ja puheensa tähden.
verses.king_james: And his brethren said to him, Shalt thou indeed reign over us? or shalt thou indeed have dominion over us? And they hated him yet the more for his dreams, and for his words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aliud quoque vidit somnium, quod narrans fratribus, ait : Vidi per somnium, quasi solem, et lunam, et stellas undecim adorare me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּחֲלֹ֥ם עוֹד֙ חֲל֣וֹם אַחֵ֔ר וַיְסַפֵּ֥ר אֹת֖וֹ לְאֶחָ֑יו וַיֹּ֗אמֶר הִנֵּ֨ה חָלַ֤מְתִּֽי חֲלוֹם֙ ע֔וֹד וְהִנֵּ֧ה הַשֶּׁ֣מֶשׁ וְהַיָּרֵ֗חַ וְאַחַ֤ד עָשָׂר֙ כּֽוֹכָבִ֔ים מִֽשְׁתַּחֲוִ֖ים לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: He dreamed also another dream, which he told his brethren, saying: I saw in a dream, as it were the sun, and the moon, and eleven stars worshipping me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän taas näki toisen unen, ja jutteli sen veljillensä, ja sanoi: katso, minä näin vielä unta: ja katso, aurinko, kuu ja yksitoista tähteä kumarsivat minua.
verses.king_james: And he dreamed yet another dream, and told it his brethren, and said, Behold, I have dreamed a dream more; and, behold, the sun and the moon and the eleven stars made obeisance to me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod cum patri suo, et fratribus retulisset, increpavit eum pater suus, et dixit : Quid sibi vult hoc somnium quod vidisti ? num ego et mater tua, et fratres tui adorabimus te super terram ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְסַפֵּ֣ר אֶל־אָבִיו֮ וְאֶל־אֶחָיו֒ וַיִּגְעַר־בּ֣וֹ אָבִ֔יו וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֔וֹ מָ֛ה הַחֲל֥וֹם הַזֶּ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֣ר חָלָ֑מְתָּ הֲב֣וֹא נָב֗וֹא אֲנִי֙ וְאִמְּךָ֣ וְאַחֶ֔יךָ לְהִשְׁתַּחֲוֺ֥ת לְךָ֖ אָֽרְצָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he had told this to his father, and brethren, his father rebuked him and said: What meaneth this dream that thou hast dreamed? shall I and thy mother, and thy brethren worship thee upon the earth?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän sitä saneli isällensä ja veljillensä, nuhteli hänen isänsä häntä ja sanoi hänelle: mikä uni se on, kuin sinä olet nähnyt? Pitääkö minun ja sinun äitis ja veljes tuleman ja kumartaman meitämme sinun edessäs maahan?
verses.king_james: And he told it to his father, and to his brethren: and his father rebuked him, and said unto him, What is this dream that thou hast dreamed? Shall I and thy mother and thy brethren indeed come to bow down ourselves to thee to the earth?

verses.latin_vulgate: Invidebant ei igitur fratres sui : pater vero rem tacitus considerabat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְקַנְאוּ־ב֖וֹ אֶחָ֑יו וְאָבִ֖יו שָׁמַ֥ר אֶת־הַדָּבָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: His brethren therefore envied him: but his father considered the thing with himself.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hänen veljensä kadehtivat häntä; mutta hänen isänsä piti nämät sanat mielessänsä.
verses.king_james: And his brethren envied him; but his father observed the saying.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque fratres illius in pascendis gregibus patris morarentur in Sichem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּלְכ֖וּ אֶחָ֑יו לִרְע֛וֹת אֶׄתׄ־צֹ֥אן אֲבִיהֶ֖ם בִּשְׁכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when his brethren abode in Sechem, feeding their father's flocks,
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska hänen veljensä menivät kaitsemaan karjaa Sikemiin,
verses.king_james: And his brethren went to feed their father’s flock in Shechem.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit ad eum Israël : Fratres tui pascunt oves in Sichimis : veni, mittam te ad eos. Quo respondente,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל אֶל־יוֹסֵ֗ף הֲל֤וֹא אַחֶ֙יךָ֙ רֹעִ֣ים בִּשְׁכֶ֔ם לְכָ֖ה וְאֶשְׁלָחֲךָ֣ אֲלֵיהֶ֑ם וַיֹּ֥אמֶר ל֖וֹ הִנֵּֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Israel said to him: Thy brethren feed the sheep in Sichem: come, I will send thee to them. And when he answered:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sanoi Israel Josephille: eikö veljes kaitse karjaa Sikemissä? Tule, minä lähetän sinun heidän tykönsä; hän vastasi: tässä minä olen.
verses.king_james: And Israel said unto Joseph, Do not thy brethren feed the flock in Shechem? come, and I will send thee unto them. And he said to him, Here am I.

verses.latin_vulgate: Præsto sum, ait ei : Vade, et vide si cuncta prospera sint erga fratres tuos, et pecora : et renuntia mihi quid agatur. Missus de valle Hebron, venit in Sichem :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ לֶךְ־נָ֨א רְאֵ֜ה אֶת־שְׁל֤וֹם אַחֶ֙יךָ֙ וְאֶת־שְׁל֣וֹם הַצֹּ֔אן וַהֲשִׁבֵ֖נִי דָּבָ֑ר וַיִּשְׁלָחֵ֙הוּ֙ מֵעֵ֣מֶק חֶבְר֔וֹן וַיָּבֹ֖א שְׁכֶֽמָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: I am ready: he said to him: Go, and see if all things be well with thy brethren, and the cattle: and bring me word again what is doing. So being sent from the vale of Hebron, he came to Sichem:
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: mene ja katso, ovatko veljes ja karja rauhassa, ja palaja minulle sitä sanomaan. Ja hän lähetti hänen Hebronin laaksosta; ja hän tuli Sikemiin.
verses.king_james: And he said to him, Go, I pray thee, see whether it be well with thy brethren, and well with the flocks; and bring me word again. So he sent him out of the vale of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.

verses.latin_vulgate: invenitque eum vir errantem in agro, et interrogavit quid quæreret.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּמְצָאֵ֣הוּ אִ֔ישׁ וְהִנֵּ֥ה תֹעֶ֖ה בַּשָּׂדֶ֑ה וַיִּשְׁאָלֵ֧הוּ הָאִ֛ישׁ לֵאמֹ֖ר מַה־תְּבַקֵּֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And a man found him there wandering in the field, and asked what he sought.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin yksi mies kohtasi hänen eksyksissä kedolla; ja kysyi häneltä sanoen: mitäs etsit?
verses.king_james: And a certain man found him, and, behold, he was wandering in the field: and the man asked him, saying, What seekest thou?

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille respondit : Fratres meos quæro : indica mihi ubi pascant greges.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר אֶת־אַחַ֖י אָנֹכִ֣י מְבַקֵּ֑שׁ הַגִּֽידָה־נָּ֣א לִ֔י אֵיפֹ֖ה הֵ֥ם רֹעִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: But he answered: I seek my brethren, tell me where they feed the flocks.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän vastasi: minä etsin veljiäni: sanos minulle, kussa he kaitsevat karjaa.
verses.king_james: And he said, I seek my brethren: tell me, I pray thee, where they feed their flocks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei vir : Recesserunt de loco isto : audivi autem eos dicentes : Eamus in Dothain. Perrexit ergo Joseph post fratres suos, et invenit eos in Dothain.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר הָאִישׁ֙ נָסְע֣וּ מִזֶּ֔ה כִּ֤י שָׁמַ֙עְתִּי֙ אֹֽמְרִ֔ים נֵלְכָ֖ה דֹּתָ֑יְנָה וַיֵּ֤לֶךְ יוֹסֵף֙ אַחַ֣ר אֶחָ֔יו וַיִּמְצָאֵ֖ם בְּדֹתָֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the man said to him: They are departed from this place: for I heard them say: Let us go to Dothain. And Joseph went forward after his brethren, and found them in Dothain.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja mies sanoi: he läksivät täältä; sillä minä kuulin heidän sanovan: käykäämme Dotaniin. Niin Joseph meni veljeinsä perään, ja löysi heidät Dotanista.
verses.king_james: And the man said, They are departed hence; for I heard them say, Let us go to Dothan. And Joseph went after his brethren, and found them in Dothan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum vidissent eum procul, antequam accederet ad eos, cogitaverunt illum occidere :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּרְא֥וּ אֹת֖וֹ מֵרָחֹ֑ק וּבְטֶ֙רֶם֙ יִקְרַ֣ב אֲלֵיהֶ֔ם וַיִּֽתְנַכְּל֥וּ אֹת֖וֹ לַהֲמִיתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when they saw him afar off, before he came nigh them, they thought to kill him:
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin he kaukana näkivät hänen, ja ennenkuin hän heitä lähestyi, pitivät he neuvoa häntä vastaan, tappaaksensa häntä.
verses.king_james: And when they saw him afar off, even before he came near unto them, they conspired against him to slay him.

verses.latin_vulgate: et mutuo loquebantur : Ecce somniator venit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֖וּ אִ֣ישׁ אֶל־אָחִ֑יו הִנֵּ֗ה בַּ֛עַל הַחֲלֹמ֥וֹת הַלָּזֶ֖ה בָּֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And said one to another: Behold the dreamer cometh.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoivat toinen toisellensa: katso, tuo unennäkiä tulee.
verses.king_james: And they said one to another, Behold, this dreamer cometh.

verses.latin_vulgate: venite, occidamus eum, et mittamus in cisternam veterem : dicemusque : Fera pessima devoravit eum : et tunc apparebit quid illi prosint somnia sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֣ה ׀ לְכ֣וּ וְנַֽהַרְגֵ֗הוּ וְנַשְׁלִכֵ֙הוּ֙ בְּאַחַ֣ד הַבֹּר֔וֹת וְאָמַ֕רְנוּ חַיָּ֥ה רָעָ֖ה אֲכָלָ֑תְהוּ וְנִרְאֶ֕ה מַה־יִּהְי֖וּ חֲלֹמֹתָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Come, let us kill him, and cast him into some old pit: and we will say: Some evil beast hath devoured him: and then it shall appear what his dreams avail him:
verses.finnish_biblia: Tulkaat siis, tappakaamme häntä, ja heittäkäämme häntä yhteen kuoppaan, ja sanokaamme: paha peto on hänen syönyt: niin saadaan nähdä, mihinkä hänen unensa joutuvat.
verses.king_james: Come now therefore, and let us slay him, and cast him into some pit, and we will say, Some evil beast hath devoured him: and we shall see what will become of his dreams.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audiens autem hoc Ruben, nitebatur liberare eum de manibus eorum, et dicebat :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֣ע רְאוּבֵ֔ן וַיַּצִּלֵ֖הוּ מִיָּדָ֑ם וַיֹּ֕אמֶר לֹ֥א נַכֶּ֖נּוּ נָֽפֶשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ruben hearing this, endeavoured to deliver him out of their hands, and said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska Ruben sen kuuli, tahtoi hän häntä pelastaa heidän käsistänsä, ja sanoi: älkäämme hänen henkeänsä ottako.
verses.king_james: And Reuben heard it, and he delivered him out of their hands; and said, Let us not kill him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non interficiatis animam ejus, nec effundatis sanguinem : sed projicite eum in cisternam hanc, quæ est in solitudine, manusque vestras servate innoxias : hoc autem dicebat, volens eripere eum de manibus eorum, et reddere patri suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אֲלֵהֶ֣ם ׀ רְאוּבֵן֮ אַל־תִּשְׁפְּכוּ־דָם֒ הַשְׁלִ֣יכוּ אֹת֗וֹ אֶל־הַבּ֤וֹר הַזֶּה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בַּמִּדְבָּ֔ר וְיָ֖ד אַל־תִּשְׁלְחוּ־ב֑וֹ לְמַ֗עַן הַצִּ֤יל אֹתוֹ֙ מִיָּדָ֔ם לַהֲשִׁיב֖וֹ אֶל־אָבִֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Do not take away his life, nor shed his blood: but cast him into this pit, that is in the wilderness, and keep your hands harmless: now he said this, being desirous to deliver him out of their hands and to restore him to his father.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Ruben vielä sanoi heille: älkäät vuodattako verta, vaan heittäkäät häntä tähän kuoppaan, joka on korvessa, ja älkäät satuttako kättänne häneen; sillä hän tahtoi pelastaa häntä heidän käsistänsä, saattaaksensa häntä jälleen isänsä tykö.
verses.king_james: And Reuben said unto them, Shed no blood, but cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness, and lay no hand upon him; that he might rid him out of their hands, to deliver him to his father again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confestim igitur ut pervenit ad fratres suos, nudaverunt eum tunica talari et polymita :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֕י כַּֽאֲשֶׁר־בָּ֥א יוֹסֵ֖ף אֶל־אֶחָ֑יו וַיַּפְשִׁ֤יטוּ אֶת־יוֹסֵף֙ אֶת־כֻּתׇּנְתּ֔וֹ אֶת־כְּתֹ֥נֶת הַפַּסִּ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And as soon as he came to his brethren, they forthwith stript him of his outside coat, that was of divers colours:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui, koska Joseph tuli veljeinsä tykö, riisuivat he häneltä hameen, (nimittäin) sen kirjavan hameen, joka hänen yllänsä oli;
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when Joseph was come unto his brethren, that they stript Joseph out of his coat, his coat of many colours that was on him;

verses.latin_vulgate: miseruntque eum in cisternam veterem, quæ non habebat aquam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֨קָּחֻ֔הוּ וַיַּשְׁלִ֥כוּ אֹת֖וֹ הַבֹּ֑רָה וְהַבּ֣וֹר רֵ֔ק אֵ֥ין בּ֖וֹ מָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And cast him into an old pit where there was not water.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ottivat hänen ja heittivät kuoppaan: ja kuoppa oli tyhjä, ja ei ollut siinä vettä.
verses.king_james: And they took him, and cast him into a pit: and the pit was empty, there was no water in it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et sedentes ut comederent panem, viderunt Ismaëlitas viatores venire de Galaad, et camelos eorum portantes aromata, et resinam, et stacten in Ægyptum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּשְׁבוּ֮ לֶֽאֱכׇל־לֶ֒חֶם֒ וַיִּשְׂא֤וּ עֵֽינֵיהֶם֙ וַיִּרְא֔וּ וְהִנֵּה֙ אֹרְחַ֣ת יִשְׁמְעֵאלִ֔ים בָּאָ֖ה מִגִּלְעָ֑ד וּגְמַלֵּיהֶ֣ם נֹֽשְׂאִ֗ים נְכֹאת֙ וּצְרִ֣י וָלֹ֔ט הוֹלְכִ֖ים לְהוֹרִ֥יד מִצְרָֽיְמָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And sitting down to eat bread, they saw some Ismaelites on their way coming from Galaad, with their camels, carrying spices, and balm, and myrrh to Egypt.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte istuivat he syömään leipää: ja kuin he nostivat silmänsä ja katselivat, niin katso, Ismaelilaisten joukko tuli Gileadista; ja heidän kameleinsa kannatus oli styraks, mastiks ja ladanum, ja vaelsivat viemään niitä alas Egyptiin.
verses.king_james: And they sat down to eat bread: and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and, behold, a company of Ishmeelites came from Gilead with their camels bearing spicery and balm and myrrh, going to carry it down to Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo Judas fratribus suis : Quid nobis prodest si occiderimus fratrem nostrum, et celaverimus sanguinem ipsius ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְהוּדָ֖ה אֶל־אֶחָ֑יו מַה־בֶּ֗צַע כִּ֤י נַהֲרֹג֙ אֶת־אָחִ֔ינוּ וְכִסִּ֖ינוּ אֶת־דָּמֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Juda said to his brethren: What will it profit us to kill our brother, and conceal his blood?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Juuda veljillensä: mitä me siitä hyödymme, että me murhaamme meidän veljemme ja salaamme hänen verensä?
verses.king_james: And Judah said unto his brethren, What profit is it if we slay our brother, and conceal his blood?

verses.latin_vulgate: melius est ut venundetur Ismaëlitis, et manus nostræ non polluantur : frater enim et caro nostra est. Acquieverunt fratres sermonibus illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְכ֞וּ וְנִמְכְּרֶ֣נּוּ לַיִּשְׁמְעֵאלִ֗ים וְיָדֵ֙נוּ֙ אַל־תְּהִי־ב֔וֹ כִּֽי־אָחִ֥ינוּ בְשָׂרֵ֖נוּ ה֑וּא וַֽיִּשְׁמְע֖וּ אֶחָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is better that he be sold to the Ismaelites, and that our hands be not defiled: for he is our brother and our flesh. His brethren agreed to his words.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tulkaat ja myykäämme häntä Ismaelilaisille, ja älkäämme satuttako käsiämme häneen: sillä hän on meidän veljemme ja lihamme; ja he kuulivat häntä.
verses.king_james: Come, and let us sell him to the Ishmeelites, and let not our hand be upon him; for he is our brother and our flesh. And his brethren were content.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et prætereuntibus Madianitis negotiatoribus, extrahentes eum de cisterna, vendiderunt eum Ismaëlitis, viginti argenteis : qui duxerunt eum in Ægyptum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּֽעַבְרוּ֩ אֲנָשִׁ֨ים מִדְיָנִ֜ים סֹֽחֲרִ֗ים וַֽיִּמְשְׁכוּ֙ וַיַּֽעֲל֤וּ אֶת־יוֹסֵף֙ מִן־הַבּ֔וֹר וַיִּמְכְּר֧וּ אֶת־יוֹסֵ֛ף לַיִּשְׁמְעֵאלִ֖ים בְּעֶשְׂרִ֣ים כָּ֑סֶף וַיָּבִ֥יאוּ אֶת־יוֹסֵ֖ף מִצְרָֽיְמָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the Madianite merchants passed by, they drew him out of the pit, and sold him to the Ismaelites, for twenty pieces of silver: and they led him into Egypt.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska Midianilaisten kauppamiehet siitä menivät ohitse, vetivät he Josephin ja ottivat ylös kuopasta, ja myivät hänen Ismaelilaisille, kahteenkymmeneen hopiapenninkiin; jotka veivät Josephin pois Egyptiin.
verses.king_james: Then there passed by Midianites merchantmen; and they drew and lifted up Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmeelites for twenty pieces of silver: and they brought Joseph into Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversusque Ruben ad cisternam, non invenit puerum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֤שׇׁב רְאוּבֵן֙ אֶל־הַבּ֔וֹר וְהִנֵּ֥ה אֵין־יוֹסֵ֖ף בַּבּ֑וֹר וַיִּקְרַ֖ע אֶת־בְּגָדָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ruben returning to the pit, found not the boy:
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska Ruben palasi kuopalle, niin katso, ei ollut Joseph kuopassa: ja hän repäisi vaatteensa
verses.king_james: And Reuben returned unto the pit; and, behold, Joseph was not in the pit; and he rent his clothes.

verses.latin_vulgate: et scissis vestibus pergens ad fratres suos, ait : Puer non comparet, et ego quo ibo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֥שׇׁב אֶל־אֶחָ֖יו וַיֹּאמַ֑ר הַיֶּ֣לֶד אֵינֶ֔נּוּ וַאֲנִ֖י אָ֥נָה אֲנִי־בָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And rending his garments he went to his brethren, and said: The boy doth not appear, and whither shall I go?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja palasi veljeinsä tykö, ja sanoi: ei ole nuorukainen siellä, voi minua! kuhunka minä menen?
verses.king_james: And he returned unto his brethren, and said, The child is not; and I, whither shall I go?

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulerunt autem tunicam ejus, et in sanguine hædi, quem occiderant, tinxerunt :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְח֖וּ אֶת־כְּתֹ֣נֶת יוֹסֵ֑ף וַֽיִּשְׁחֲטוּ֙ שְׂעִ֣יר עִזִּ֔ים וַיִּטְבְּל֥וּ אֶת־הַכֻּתֹּ֖נֶת בַּדָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they took his coat, and dipped it in the blood of a kid, which they had killed:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin he ottivat Josephin hameen ja tappoivat kauriin, ja tahrasivat hameen vereen
verses.king_james: And they took Joseph’s coat, and killed a kid of the goats, and dipped the coat in the blood;

verses.latin_vulgate: mittentes qui ferrent ad patrem, et dicerent : Hanc invenimus : vide utrum tunica filii tui sit, an non.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְשַׁלְּח֞וּ אֶת־כְּתֹ֣נֶת הַפַּסִּ֗ים וַיָּבִ֙יאוּ֙ אֶל־אֲבִיהֶ֔ם וַיֹּאמְר֖וּ זֹ֣את מָצָ֑אנוּ הַכֶּר־נָ֗א הַכְּתֹ֧נֶת בִּנְךָ֛ הִ֖וא אִם־לֹֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Sending some to carry it to their father, and to say: This we have found: see whether it be thy son's coat, or not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja lähettivät sen kirjavan hameen, ja antoivat kantaa isällensä, ja sanoivat: tämän me löysimme: tunnustele, jos tämä on poikas hame taikka ei?
verses.king_james: And they sent the coat of many colours, and they brought it to their father; and said, This have we found: know now whether it be thy son’s coat or no.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam cum agnovisset pater, ait : Tunica filii mei est : fera pessima comedit eum, bestia devoravit Joseph.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּכִּירָ֤הּ וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ כְּתֹ֣נֶת בְּנִ֔י חַיָּ֥ה רָעָ֖ה אֲכָלָ֑תְהוּ טָרֹ֥ף טֹרַ֖ף יוֹסֵֽף׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the father acknowledging it, said: It is my son's coat, an evil wild beast hath eaten him, a beast hath devoured Joseph.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän tunsi sen, ja sanoi: tämä on minun poikani hame, paha peto on hänen syönyt: raadellen on Joseph revelty.
verses.king_james: And he knew it, and said, It is my son’s coat; an evil beast hath devoured him; Joseph is without doubt rent in pieces.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scissisque vestibus, indutus est cilicio, lugens filium suum multo tempore.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרַ֤ע יַעֲקֹב֙ שִׂמְלֹתָ֔יו וַיָּ֥שֶׂם שַׂ֖ק בְּמׇתְנָ֑יו וַיִּתְאַבֵּ֥ל עַל־בְּנ֖וֹ יָמִ֥ים רַבִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And tearing his garments, he put on sackcloth, mourning for his son a long time.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob repäisi vaatteensa, ja kääri säkin kupeisiinsa; ja murehti poikaansa monta päivää.
verses.king_james: And Jacob rent his clothes, and put sackcloth upon his loins, and mourned for his son many days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Congregatis autem cunctis liberis ejus ut lenirent dolorem patris, noluit consolationem accipere, sed ait : Descendam ad filium meum lugens in infernum. Et illo perseverante in fletu,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּקֻ֩מוּ֩ כׇל־בָּנָ֨יו וְכׇל־בְּנֹתָ֜יו לְנַחֲמ֗וֹ וַיְמָאֵן֙ לְהִתְנַחֵ֔ם וַיֹּ֕אמֶר כִּֽי־אֵרֵ֧ד אֶל־בְּנִ֛י אָבֵ֖ל שְׁאֹ֑לָה וַיֵּ֥בְךְּ אֹת֖וֹ אָבִֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all his children being gathered together to comfort their father in his sorrow, he would not receive comfort, but said: I will go down to my son into hell, mourning. And whilst he continued weeping,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki hänen poikansa ja tyttärensä nousivat häntä lohduttamaan; vaan ei hän tahtonut antaa itsiänsä lohdutettaa, mutta sanoi: minä menen murehtien alas poikani tykö hautaan. Ja hänen isänsä itki häntä.
verses.king_james: And all his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him; but he refused to be comforted; and he said, For I will go down into the grave unto my son mourning. Thus his father wept for him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Madianitæ vendiderunt Joseph in Ægypto Putiphari eunucho Pharaonis, magistro militum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַ֨מְּדָנִ֔ים מָכְר֥וּ אֹת֖וֹ אֶל־מִצְרָ֑יִם לְפֽוֹטִיפַר֙ סְרִ֣יס פַּרְעֹ֔ה שַׂ֖ר הַטַּבָּחִֽים׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: The Madianites sold Joseph in Egypt to Putiphar, an eunuch of Pharao, captain of the soldiers.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Midianilaiset myivät hänen Egyptiin Potipharille, Pharaon kamaripalvelialle ja huovinhaltialle.
verses.king_james: And the Midianites sold him into Egypt unto Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh’s, and captain of the guard.
